scriptId: cs_v4_NOISE_L40_Excessive phone or social media use
historyId: h_v4_NOISE_L40_Excessive phone or social media use

goal: Cut back on excessive phone and social media use; improve sleep and focus.
constraints: Keep each action to no more than fifteen words.
Avoid recommending named apps, subscriptions, or brand-specific tools.
decisions: Alternate with physical books; pause to jot one takeaway.
Try grayscale mode for a week.
Change wallpaper to plain, low-stimulation to reduce visual pull.
entities: no phone for first 30 minutes after waking
phone out of reach during work
screens off one hour before bed
open: Put phone in another room or a closed drawer during focus.
Remove social apps from home screen or log out temporarily.
Use a physical kitchen timer for work/break cycles; keep phone away.
Pre-commit to a friend: ask them to check in if you slip.
When the urge hits, do a 2-minute substitute (walk, stretch, drink water).
Turn the screen grayscale and silence banners to reduce visual pull.
Address stress-driven app openings; add friction and delay.
Enable Do Not Disturb with exceptions for important contacts.
Forward only high-priority channels to smartwatch; mute social feeds.
Use smartwatch vibration for essentials; require phone opening for full content.
Try smartwatch setup for a few days; monitor wrist-checks vs phone pickups.
Before leaving: place phone zipped face-down in bag; keep paperback or downloaded chapter ready.
Start the commute by opening your book immediately; no phone unlocking until arrival.
Choose short reads—articles, short stories, or single chapters—for easy, satisfying completion.
If the urge hits, read one page or take three deep breaths before checking.
Silence non-essentials during commute; allow calls/messages from 1–2 contacts.
